query: As of early July 2026, what percentage of Russia's national oil-refining capacity is offline/disabled due to Ukrainian drone strikes?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

## Answer: ~25% of Russia's national oil-refining capacity is offline as of early July 2026

**Best single-number estimate: ~25% (a quarter)**, within a defensible range of **~20–28%**.

### Key findings across sources:
- **~25%** — Russian expert Stanislav Mitrakhovich (Al Jazeera, July 2, 2026) — most current data point
- **"More than 20%"** — RFE/RL, noting IEA called it "unprecedented"
- **"Up to 28%"** (peak) — CEPA and Carnegie, following mid-June strikes
- **"~one-third"** — TechTimes (high-end outlier)

### Trajectory:
The figure escalated sharply from ~17% in late 2025 (Reuters baseline) through Q2 2026, driven by an intensified drone campaign culminating in the mid-June 2026 strikes on Moscow-area refineries (Kapotnya, TANECO), which alone removed ~600,000 bpd.

### Scope of targeting:
- 8 of Russia's 10 largest refineries hit
- 12 major European-Russia refineries struck; only Omsk and Angarsk (east of the Urals) unhit
- Triggered fuel rationing in over half of Russia's 83 regions, a gasoline export ban, and a Crimean state of emergency

### Key caveats:
- "Offline" blurs full halts vs. reduced output
- The percentage fluctuates week-to-week as refineries are repaired and re-struck
- Russia restricts refinery data, so true degradation may be understated
- Note: the ~40% figure sometimes cited refers to oil **export** capacity (Reuters, March 2026), which is distinct from refining capacity

The full structured findings, sources, and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: 'The crisis is deep': The view from Russia as fuel shortages worsen
sources.snippet: Ukrainian drone attacks have disabled about a quarter of Russia's oil refining capacity, per Stanislav Mitrakhovich, National Energy Security Fund.

sources.title: Russia Is Grappling With Its Worst Nationwide Fuel Shortages In Years. Here's Where.
sources.snippet: More than 20 percent of Russia's total refining capacity has been knocked offline; 8 of Russia's 10 largest refineries hit; IEA called disruption 'unprecedented'.

sources.title: Running on Empty: Russia's Fuel Crisis - CEPA
sources.snippet: Up to 28% of refining capacity lost following mid-June strikes; 8 of Russia's 10 largest refineries affected; ~13% decline April-May from 5.2M bpd baseline.

sources.title: Russian Oil Sector Battered but Not Broken by Ukrainian Air Attacks
sources.snippet: Mid-June attacks on Moscow (Kapotnya) and TANECO knocked out ~600,000 bpd; capacity loss might be 28% down on previous years; Ryazan attacked 15 times.

sources.title: Ukrainian drones have struck nearly every major Russian refinery. Which facilities have yet to be hit?
sources.snippet: 12 major refineries in European Russia struck; only Omsk and Angarsk (east of Urals) not yet hit.

sources.title: Ukraine Drone Blitz Burns Moscow Refinery: One-Third of Russian Refining Goes Offline
sources.snippet: June 18, 2026 largest drone offensive on Moscow burned Gazprom Neft refinery; ~one-third of national refining capacity offline (high-end outlier estimate).

sources.title: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Halt at Least 40% of Russia's Oil Export Capacity - Reuters
sources.snippet: ~40% of Russia's oil EXPORT capacity halted (March 2026); distinct from refining capacity.

sources.title: Reuters: Ukrainian Strikes on 10 Oil Refineries Knock Out 17% of Russia's Refining Capacity
sources.snippet: Late 2025 baseline: strikes on 10 refineries knocked out at least 17% (~1.1M bpd) of refining capacity.

sources.title: 2025-2026 Russian fuel crisis - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Crisis escalated mid-2026; Crimea declared state of emergency and banned fuel sales in June 2026.
